The issue arises from the handling of th:
Handle Expressions in For-In Loops
Check if the lhs of the for-in statement is an expression. If so, identify the variable being assigned and add it to the GEN and KILL sets instead of the entire expression. This could involve adding a check for NodeUtil.isExpression(lhs) and then handling the expression to extract the variable.

Modify addToSetIfLocal Method
Modify the addToSetIfLocal method to handle expressions by checking if the node is an expression and if so, extract the variable being assigned and add it to the set. This would require adding a check for expressions and then recursively searching for the variable within the expression to add to the set.

Introduce Precondition Checks for Simple Variables
Before calling addToSetIfLocal, introduce precondition checks to ensure that the node is a simple variable. If not, handle the expression accordingly to avoid the IllegalStateException. This could involve adding a check like if (NodeUtil.isName(node)) before calling addToSetIfLocal to ensure that only simple variables are passed to the method.
